    "options": {
      "A": "Creep resistance is stronger in the longitudinal orientation than in the transverse orientation.",
      "B": "The longitudinal condition is associated with fewer slip bands and SISF-related deformation.",
      "C": "The transverse condition activates more slip systems and APB shearing.",
      "D": "The longitudinal condition involves APB shearing.",
      "E": "The transverse condition is dominated by SISF-related deformation.",
      "F": "Both orientations exhibit identical creep resistance."
    },
    "answer": ["A", "D", "E"],
    "answer_aliases": [],
    "reference_answer": "The longitudinal orientation exhibits stronger creep resistance, activates more slip systems and APB shearing. The transverse orientation is dominated by fewer slip bands and SISF-related deformation.",
